title: parametric-plot widget params
description: Shared widget module for the slider-plot / slider-plot-actions / select-slider-plot families (combined ~159 widgets, the largest cluster in the corpus per audits/widget-families.md). A parametric-plot widget renders a header, an SVG host, one or more <input type='range'> sliders (each paired with a <label> and a live-updating <span class='small'>), optional action buttons, and a readout div. The per-widget draw logic varies too much to generalize without loss, so the IIFE body is carried as a `bodyScript` artifact string — a portable consumer should ignore `bodyScript` and drive its own renderer from the structural params. The `oneOf` dispatches between `single-mode` (bare slider-plot: sliders + optional buttons) and `multi-mode` (select-slider-plot: a <select> picks a mode, each mode supplies its own sliderDefaults + draw).

outId.description: Optional DOM id for the adjacent <span class='small'> that echoes the current value. Omit for sliders with no live echo.

label.description: Inner HTML of the <label for=...>: may contain $…$ LaTeX.

initReadout.description: Initial text for the <span class='small' id=outId> (e.g. 't = 0.50').

labelWraps.description: When true, the <input> nests INSIDE the <label> as `<label>labelText <input ...></label>`; the `for=` attribute is omitted. When false/absent, emits the default sibling form `<label for=id>labelText</label><input ...>`.

svgTitle.description: <title> child of the <svg>. Defaults to the widget's title if omitted.

outputInitial.description: Initial inner HTML of the readout <div>. Defaults to ' ' to match the slider-plot convention.

buttons.description: Optional action buttons rendered after the slider rows (slider-plot-actions family).

sectionComment.description: ARTIFACT. Raw text between the leading <script> tag and the IIFE opener, emitted as `/* <sectionComment> */`. Preserves the legacy source's /* ========== Section N: … ========== */ banners.

bodyScript.x-artifact: script body is widget-specific; portable consumers should implement their own draw logic
bodyScript.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS statements that go inside `(function(){\n…\n})();` — reads sliders, renders SVG, writes readout. Kept opaque because the 81+ widgets in this family each compute bespoke geometry; a React/Three.js consumer should ignore this field and drive its own renderer from sliders + any future structured `draw` params.

sliders.description: Sliders shared across modes; each mode can override min/max/step/init via modes[value].sliderDefaults.

modes.description: Portable per-mode data, keyed by option value. Portable consumers read sliderDefaults and draw; artifact consumers fall through to bodyScript.

sliderDefaults.description: Per-slider overrides of { min, max, step, init } applied when this mode is selected.

draw.description: Optional structured draw descriptor (kind + data) for portable renderers. Artifact consumers ignore this and rely on bodyScript.

initialMode.description: Option value the IIFE starts on.

bodyScript.x-artifact: script body is widget-specific; portable consumers should implement their own
bodyScript.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim IIFE body. Same rationale as singleMode.bodyScript.
